sourceMovie.title: A Fantastic Fear of Everything
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: A Fantastic Fear of Everything works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The 'Burbs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Fantastic Fear of Everything clicked for you, The 'Burbs (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ghostland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Fantastic Fear of Everything clicked for you, Ghostland (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Fantastic Fear of Everything?
faq.answer: Try The 'Burbs, Ghostland, The Voices—each shares DNA with A Fantastic Fear of Everything while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Fantastic Fear of Everything?
faq.answer: A Fantastic Fear of Everything (2012) is best described as Comedy, Thriller, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Fantastic Fear of Everything worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
